hypothesis: CVE-2025-49132 LFI to RCE via pearcmd.php
action: two-stage: config-create webshell + LFI include

hypothesis: CVE-2025-6018 PAM bypass gives polkit allow_active via .pam_environment XDG_SEAT/XDG_VTNR injection
action: set .pam_environment with OVERRIDE format, reconnect SSH, check CanReboot
why: polkit-default-privs package installs 90-default-privs.rules which likely overrides implicit allow_active=yes. PAM bypass sets seat0/active correctly but custom JS rule blocks it. Need to read and understand the rule to find bypass.

hypothesis: polkit-default-privs restrictive profile blocks allow_active for udisks2
action: read /etc/sysconfig/security PERMISSION_SECURITY and polkit-default-privs profiles
why: PERMISSION_SECURITY=easy local → standard profile → loop-setup allow_active=yes. Profile does NOT restrict active users. Something else in 90-default-privs.rules JS must be adding extra checks (possibly Remote session check). Need to read the generated JS file or the Perl generator.

hypothesis: chkstat-polkit Perl script generates JS rule that checks Remote flag
action: need to read /sbin/chkstat-polkit to see generated JS template

hypothesis: polkit JS rule 90-default-privs.rules checks subject.local which is false for Remote=yes sessions
action: read chkstat-polkit perl script, found JS template uses subject.local for index selection
evidence: if (subject.local) { i=2 for active } else i=0 → auth_admin. Remote=yes means subject.local=false → auth_admin always.

hypothesis: su from wwwrun RCE to phileasfogg3 creates local session with Remote=false
action: wrote check script, ran via su from wwwrun, checked sd_session_is_remote

hypothesis: create XFS image on target, mount via udisksd, inject SUID bash
action: ran exploit script via su from wwwrun
why: cp failed with permission denied on udisksd-mounted fs (root owns mount point). Also loop device name not captured properly for resize. Need to inject SUID binary INTO image without mounting, or mount with write access.

hypothesis: CVE-2025-6019 XFS resize race via local session su from wwwrun - fixed XFS ownership and loop device handling
action: modified root inode uid/gid via xfs_db, rewrote exploit script with proper loop device capture, running via su -l from wwwrun RCE

hypothesis: CVE-2025-6019 XFS resize race with root-owned SUID bash in image, 20 parallel resize attempts
action: xfs_db to set uid=0 gid=0 mode=0104755, parallel gdbus resize calls

hypothesis: XFS resize was no-op because filesystem filled entire image - need undersized XFS
action: creating 200MB XFS in 300MB container file via dd append
why: file shows data not XFS after append, xfs_db needs -F flag to force read on extended file, testing now

hypothesis: XFS resize needs filesystem smaller than loop device for actual grow to trigger temp mount
action: extending 300MB XFS image to 500MB via truncate to create growable gap
why: mkfs.xfs min 300MB on this system, using truncate to extend file past FS boundary. Verifying image integrity now.

hypothesis: extend xfs.img to 500MB via truncate to create growable space for resize race
action: cp xfs.img xfs6.img then truncate -s 500M
why: having trouble verifying if xfs6.img was created - output parsing issues. Need to verify and then inject SUID binary and re-run race.

hypothesis: CVE-2025-6019 resize race with 500MB image (300MB FS + 200MB growth room)
action: extended xfs.img to 500MB via truncate, ran exploit_final.sh via su from wwwrun
why: output file appears empty or unreadable. The exploit may have timed out or the su command failed. Need to debug output capture. The race condition may also need more attempts or different timing.

hypothesis: Run CVE-2025-6019 exploit via SSH+su for local session
action: expect script with SSH+su combo
why: Two issues: (1) /tmp full from 500MB xfs.img + other files causing no space left. (2) su from SSH session returns sd_session_is_remote=-61 (ENODATA/no session) - su from SSH doesnt create new logind session. Only su from wwwrun RCE creates local session. Need to clean disk, recreate webshell, run exploit via wwwrun RCE su.

hypothesis: Recreate webshell and use /dev/shm for XFS image to avoid disk space issues
action: freed 301MB by deleting old images, recreated webshell at /tmp/cmd.php, plan to create XFS image on /dev/shm (987MB tmpfs)

hypothesis: Run exploit from /dev/shm with local session via su from wwwrun
action: su -l phileasfogg3 -c exploit.sh, image on /dev/shm
why: udisksctl loop-setup failed silently - LOOP_DEV empty. Need to check actual error. Possible: /dev/shm path restricted by udisksd, or polkit issue with the su session.

hypothesis: su from wwwrun creates local session (worked before)
action: re-tested su session check
why: ret=-61 ENODATA - no session created. Likely because existing SSH sessions for phileasfogg3 cause pam_systemd to create scope instead of new session. Need to kill all SSH sessions first, or find another way to get a clean session.

hypothesis: su from wwwrun stopped creating sessions - may be due to DISPLAY in pam_environment or lingering session state
action: checking pam_environment content and session state

hypothesis: su from php-fpm.service stays in system.slice - pam_systemd doesnt create session for su from service context
action: checked cgroup of su process - stays in php-fpm.service

hypothesis: Need to kill zombie sessions then immediately run exploit in same su that creates new local session
action: killed c107 then ran exploit but session was already gone by then
why: Killing the session also kills the su context. Need to ensure no zombie sessions exist BEFORE running su, then let su create fresh session and run exploit.

hypothesis: exploit fails because udisksctl loop-setup fails in su session
action: ran exploit script, P1 MOUNT FAILED
why: loop-setup returning empty output. Need to capture actual error from udisksctl.

hypothesis: exploit script loop-setup fails but standalone succeeds - parsing or session issue in script
action: ran exploit script via su, P1 LOOP empty
why: checking p1.log for actual udisksctl error

hypothesis: CVE-2025-6019 XFS resize race - script works via bash -x, Phase 1 succeeds, Phase 3 resize runs but no /tmp/blockdev.* mount appears
action: ran exploit with bash -x debug, full output captured
why: Phase 1 OK (loop+mount+cp+unmount+xfs_db). Phase 3 resize runs but race watcher never finds /tmp/blockdev.*/xpl. Need to check: (1) did resize actually create temp mount? (2) is the mount at a different path? (3) is the race window too small?

hypothesis: CVE-2025-6019 resize created temp mount at /tmp/blockdev.8BEPN3 without nosuid - mount persisted because unmount failed (target busy)
action: found active mount via mount command
evidence: /dev/shm/xfs.img on /tmp/blockdev.8BEPN3 type xfs (rw,relatime,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,noquota) - NO nosuid flag! Now need to check if xpl exists with SUID root.

hypothesis: CVE-2025-6019 XFS resize race gives root via SUID bash on nosuid-free temp mount
action: xpl -p -c id from /tmp/blockdev.8BEPN3

hypothesis: LDAP deep search for unusual attributes on domain objects
conclusion: No objects found with unixUserPassword, userPassword, info, comment, wWWHomePage, or personalTitle attributes set

hypothesis: GPO modification by j.arbuckle
conclusion: Only 2 default GPOs found (Default Domain Policy, Default Domain Controllers Policy). Need to check ACLs with dacledit or similar.

hypothesis: ADCS certificate templates exist
conclusion: No certificate templates found, no enrollment services (CAs) configured. ADCS is truly absent.

hypothesis: User accounts with SPNs (Kerberoastable)
conclusion: Only krbtgt has SPN (kadmin/changepw). No kerberoastable user accounts.

hypothesis: RODC01 managedBy attribute set
conclusion: No managedBy attribute on RODC01$. BUT: msDS-RevealedUsers shows Administrator AND krbtgt_8245 credentials have been cached/revealed to RODC. This is a CRITICAL finding - if we can compromise RODC or its krbtgt_8245, we can forge RODC golden tickets.

hypothesis: WPAD record exists in ADIDNS
conclusion: No WPAD record exists. WPAD record can be created by j.arbuckle (Everyone has CREATE_CHILD on zone). This enables LLMNR/WPAD poisoning attacks via DNS.

hypothesis: l.wilson_adm has elevated privileges via Tier 1 group
conclusion: l.wilson_adm is member of: Tier 1, Remote Management Users, Remote Desktop Users. Can WinRM/RDP to DC. No adminCount set. Need to check what Tier 1 has ACLs on.

hypothesis: Research: check logon scripts, IT Support group
conclusion: Both l.wilson and l.wilson_adm have scriptPath=printerDetect.bat. l.wilson is also in Remote Management Users + Remote Desktop Users. IT Support group has j.arbuckle. Allowed RODC Replication Group is EMPTY but Administrator is in RevealedUsers anyway (may have been manually replicated).

hypothesis: Logon scripts contain sensitive information
conclusion: CRITICAL: reset.vbs in SYSVOL sets l.wilson_adm password to H4ck3d!@2026 on every logon. printerDetect.bat also does dir \\10.10.17.114\share (hardcoded IP). l.wilson_adm is in Tier 1 + Remote Management Users + Remote Desktop Users. Can WinRM to DC.

hypothesis: Tier 1 has ACLs on DC01
conclusion: No ACLs for Tier 1 on DC01$.

hypothesis: Tier 1 has ACLs on krbtgt_8245 or RODC01
conclusion: No ACLs for Tier 1 on krbtgt_8245 or RODC01$.

hypothesis: IT Support has write ACLs on user objects
conclusion: CRITICAL: IT Support (j.arbuckle) has WriteProperty on scriptPath attribute for ALL user objects (inherited ACE). This means j.arbuckle can modify the logon script of ANY user including l.wilson_adm. Combined with the reset.vbs finding, this is the attack path: modify scriptPath or write a malicious script to SYSVOL.

hypothesis: Delegation check
conclusion: DC01$ has unconstrained delegation (standard for DCs). No other delegation found.

hypothesis: Tier 1 has write ACLs on admin groups
conclusion: Tier 1 has NO ACLs on Administrators, Domain Admins, Denied/Allowed RODC Replication Groups, DC01$, RODC01$, or krbtgt_8245. Tier 1 membership gives: Remote Management Users + Remote Desktop Users access (WinRM/RDP to DC).

hypothesis: j.arbuckle can write to SYSVOL scripts directory
conclusion: CRITICAL: j.arbuckle has WRITE access to SYSVOL/garfield.htb/scripts/. Can upload malicious scripts. Combined with scriptPath WriteProperty ACL, full attack chain available.
